entries.content:
Committed on `josephhart/joe-7-add-rainbow-colored-snake-suprise`:

```
a186ad2 Add Rainbow Snake surprise (JOE-7)
 4 files changed, 192 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
```

The `punycode` deprecation warning is just a Node notice (from a git hook/tooling) — harmless, the commit went through cleanly.

The branch isn't pushed yet. Want me to push it and open a PR? Per your setup I'd use the `jolli-pr` skill to generate the description from memory.
When does the rainbow banner apppear and for how long?

I'll look at the rainbow banner code to answer precisely.

I'll look at the banner logic in `src/main.ts`.

Let me read the exact banner show/hide logic and where it's triggered.

Here's exactly how the rainbow banner is timed.

**When it appears:** At the start of a game (`startNewGame`), but only on a *rainbow game* — rolled once per game with a 1-in-100 chance (`RAINBOW_CHANCE = 0.01`). The roll holds for the whole run, so the banner shows the moment that rare game begins. You can force it with `?rainbow=1` in the URL (or suppress it with `?rainbow=0`) for testing.

**For how long:** It pops in and holds fully visible for **3 seconds** (`RAINBOW_BANNER_MS = 3000`), then fades out over **600 ms** (`RAINBOW_BANNER_FADE_MS = 600`) before being hidden — so **~3.6 seconds** total on screen (`src/main.ts:2309-2318`).

It also auto-dismisses early if you leave the game view — `showScreen` calls `hideRainbowBanner()` whenever the screen isn't `game` (`src/main.ts:2651-2652`), so a quick death won't leave it lingering over another screen. The rainbow snake coloring itself, though, persists for the entire run regardless of the banner.

let's add a seperate hidden leaderboard for scores made during a rainbow game.  you can only see that leaderboard if there was a score previously entered there.  otherwise it will remain hidden.  make a plan first

I'll research how the existing leaderboard works before planning. Let me explore the code and recall any prior decisions.

Let me read the leaderboard module and the key UI wiring in detail.

Let me read the game-over/entry flow and the tab-rendering logic in detail.

Now the entry-prompt and leaderboard rendering/tab logic.

Let me check whether rainbow rolls in all game modes and whether anything iterates exhaustively over modes.

I now have the full picture of how the leaderboard system works. Two product decisions genuinely change the implementation, so let me confirm those before writing the plan.

Decisions noted: **all modes** feed the hidden board, and rainbow scores go to the **rainbow board only** (not the normal board). Let me check the existing test patterns so the plan's test section is accurate.

I've got the full picture. Here's my plan.

## Plan: Hidden "Rainbow Snake" leaderboard

### Behavior
- A **4th leaderboard mode, `rainbow`**, collects every score made during a rainbow game — across all modes (single, PvP, BvB), matching your choices.
- Rainbow-game scores go to the **rainbow board only** — they're rerouted away from the normal single/pvp/bvb boards. The routing rule is one line: `const target = this.rainbowSnake ? 'rainbow' : <normalMode>`.
- The rainbow tab is **hidden until the board has ≥1 entry** — a pure data-driven reveal (`getLeaderboard('rainbow').length > 0`). Clearing it re-hides it.

### Changes

**1. `src/leaderboard.ts`**
- Add `'rainbow'` to `LeaderboardMode`.
- Add `MODE_LABELS.rainbow = '🌈 Rainbow Snake'` (TS will force this once the type changes).
- No storage changes needed — it reuses the existing `snake-leaderboard-v1:rainbow` key mechanism for free.

**2. `index.html`**
- Add a 4th tab `<button class="lb-tab" data-mode="rainbow" hidden>🌈 Rainbow</button>` in `#leaderboard-tabs`, hidden by default.

**3. `src/main.ts`**
- **Routing (3 sites):** In `endGame` (single), `endGameTwoPlayer` (pvp branch and bvb branch), compute the target mode as `'rainbow'` when `this.rainbowSnake`, else the current mode. Existing disqualification rules stay intact (bot-assisted single runs still don't prompt; bvb still auto-records both snakes — now to the rainbow board when it was a rainbow game).
- **Reveal:** New `updateRainbowTabVisibility()` toggles `hidden` on the rainbow tab from the board's entry count. Call it in `openLeaderboards()`.
- **Open guard:** New helper so `openLeaderboards('rainbow')` / `view-leaderboard` fall back to `'single'` if the rainbow board is empty (prevents landing on a board whose tab is hidden — e.g. a stale `leaderboardActiveMode` after a clear).
- **Nice reveal:** `view-leaderboard` opens the rainbow board when the just-finished run was a rainbow game (`this.rainbowSnake`), so the player discovers the secret board right after scoring on it.
- **Clear handling:** After "Clear This Board" on the rainbow board, re-hide the tab and switch the active mode to `'single'`.
- **Mixed-columns rendering:** Since the rainbow board aggregates single/pvp/bvb entries, `renderLeaderboardTable` shows the Result column if any entry has `result`, and Opponent if any has `opponent` (instead of the current mode-based flags).

**4. `src/style.css`**
- Small rainbow-gradient flair on the `[data-mode="rainbow"]` tab so the reveal feels special.

**5. `tests/e2e/rainbow.spec.ts`** (extend)
- Rainbow tab hidden on a fresh context (open leaderboards from menu, assert the rainbow tab is hidden).
- Drive a `?rainbow=1` single-player run into the wall → game-over prompt → enter a name → save; then assert the rainbow tab is now visible, the rainbow board contains the entry, and the **single** board does **not**.
- `?rainbow=0` run that scores does not reveal the rainbow tab.

### Notes / edge cases handled
- Even a score of 0 qualifies on a non-full board, so any rainbow run that finishes reveals the board (consistent with existing single-player behavior).
- Rainbow visual only recolors P1, but "score during a rainbow game" counts whichever snake scores (PvP winner, both BvB bots) — the whole game is the rainbow game.

Want me to go ahead and implement this?

Now the HTML tab:

Now the routing in `main.ts`. First, the single-player branch:

Now the BvB branch (both auto-recorded entries) and the PvP branch:

Now the leaderboard UI: add the routing/reveal helpers and update `openLeaderboards`:

Now the mixed-columns rendering:

Now the event handlers — clear (recompute visibility/fallback) and view-leaderboard (reveal the board after a rainbow run):

Now the CSS flair for the rainbow tab. Let me read the existing tab styles:

Now add the rainbow tab flair:

Now let me extend the e2e tests. I'll rewrite `rainbow.spec.ts` to add the hidden-leaderboard coverage alongside the existing banner tests:

Now let me typecheck and run the build, then the rainbow e2e tests.

Typecheck passes. Now run the rainbow e2e specs:

All 5 rainbow specs pass. Let me run the full suite to confirm nothing regressed:

All 13 pass. Let me run the production build to confirm it compiles clean end-to-end:

Done. The hidden Rainbow Snake leaderboard is implemented, typechecks, builds, and all 13 e2e tests pass (including 3 new ones for the feature).

## What changed

**`src/leaderboard.ts`** — Added `'rainbow'` to `LeaderboardMode` and a `🌈 Rainbow Snake` label. The board reuses the existing storage machinery (`snake-leaderboard-v1:rainbow`), so `getLeaderboard`/`addEntry`/`qualifies`/`clearLeaderboard` all work on it unchanged.

**`index.html`** — Added a 4th tab `data-mode="rainbow"` that's `hidden` by default.

**`src/main.ts`**
- **Routing** — one helper, `leaderboardTargetMode(normal)`, returns `'rainbow'` when the run was a rainbow game, else the normal mode. Applied at all three scoring sites (single prompt, PvP prompt, both BvB auto-records), so rainbow scores go to the secret board *only* — never the normal one. All modes feed it, per your choice.
- **Reveal** — `updateRainbowTabVisibility()` shows the tab only when the board has ≥1 entry; called on every leaderboard open. `openLeaderboards` guards against landing on the board while empty (falls back to Single Player), so a stale/cleared state can't strand you on a hidden board.
- **Discovery touch** — after a rainbow run, *View Leaderboard* opens straight to the newly-revealed board.
- **Clearing** the rainbow board re-hides its tab and drops you back to a visible board.
- **Rendering** — the rainbow board aggregates single/pvp/bvb entries, so its Result/Opponent columns now appear based on what the entries actually carry.

**`src/style.css`** — A rainbow-gradient sheen on the secret tab so the reveal reads as a prize.

**`tests/e2e/rainbow.spec.ts`** — Three new specs: the tab stays hidden on a fresh context; a `?rainbow=1` run routes its score to the secret board, reveals the tab, and stays *off* the Single Player board; a `?rainbow=0` run records normally and keeps the tab hidden.

One behavior worth flagging: bot-assisted *single-player* runs remain disqualified from scoring entirely (as before) — they don't record to either board. Only genuine Bot-vs-Bot runs auto-record (now to the rainbow board when it was a rainbow game), matching the existing leaderboard integrity rule.

I'll commit the changes. Let me check the status and stage the relevant files.

All five files are part of this feature, and we're already on the feature branch. Committing:
